# GitHub Copilot Instructions â€” CI Ready Maven

## Project Overview

CI Ready Maven is a static-site catalogue of Maven plugins and utilities for enterprise Java CI pipelines and quality gates. The site is generated by `build.java` (a JBang script) which reads Markdown files from the repository root and writes HTML to `_site/`.

## Adding a Catalogue Entry

Create a new Markdown file in the repository root (e.g. `my-plugin.md`) with YAML front matter followed by a description and a `## Code Example` section.

### Required Front Matter Fields

```yaml
---
name: Human-readable display name
category: One of the canonical categories (see below)
type: Maven Plugin | CLI Tool | Maven Plugin / CLI Tool
mavenCoordinates: groupId:artifactId
lastRelease: "x.y.z"
learnMoreText: Link label shown in the UI
learnMoreHref: https://...
tags:
    - Tag One
    - Tag Two
dateAdded: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

All fields are required. `lastRelease` must be a quoted string.

### Canonical Categories

Use one of these exact strings for `category`:

- `Coverage`
- `Dependency Governance`
- `Security and Compliance`
- `Source Code Policy`
- `Static Analysis`

### Tagging System

Tags are used for filtering and grouping in the UI. Each entry should have **at least one** tag. Use existing tags wherever possible to avoid tag proliferation. Add a new tag only when no existing tag fits.

#### Existing Tags

| Tag | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `Build Reproducibility` | Tools that enforce deterministic, reproducible builds (e.g. dependency convergence, version pinning) |
| `Coverage` | Test coverage measurement and enforcement |
| `Dependency Governance` | Managing, auditing, and constraining third-party dependencies |
| `Formatting` | Source code formatting and style enforcement |
| `Security and Compliance` | Vulnerability scanning, license checks, supply chain security |
| `Source Code Policy` | Broad source quality policies including formatting, structure, and naming |
| `Source Structure` | Enforcing the physical layout and ordering of code within source files |
| `Static Analysis` | Bytecode or source-level bug detection and code quality rules |

#### Tag Assignment Guidelines

- A tool may have multiple tags when it genuinely covers several concerns.
- `category` represents the primary concern; `tags` can be broader.
- Tools in the `Source Code Policy` category should always carry at least one of `Formatting` or `Source Structure` in addition to the `Source Code Policy` tag.
- Tools in `Security and Compliance` should carry `Dependency Governance` if they analyse third-party dependencies.

## Body Content

The body of each Markdown file must include:

1. **A description paragraph** â€“ concise prose (3â€“6 sentences) explaining what the tool does, why it matters in a CI context, and any key capabilities.
2. **`## Code Example`** â€“ a fenced XML code block with a minimal, copy-paste-ready `<plugin>` configuration.  
   - Use the `check`/`verify` goal (not the fix/format goal) for the CI-appropriate default.
   - Include a comment in the snippet if both a fix goal and a check goal exist, e.g. `<!-- Use 'format' goal to auto-fix, 'check' goal for CI -->`.

## Build Process

The static site is generated with JBang:

```bash
jbang run build.java
```

This reads all `*.md` files in the root (except `README.md`), parses their YAML front matter and Markdown body, then writes individual tool pages, grid partials, tag pages, and `index.html` into `_site/`. Run this command after every change to regenerate the site.

The site is deployed automatically via GitHub Actions on every push to `main`.

## UI Architecture

- **`build.java`** â€“ sole build script; uses [j2html](https://j2html.com/) for HTML generation, [CommonMark](https://github.com/commonmark/commonmark-java) for Markdown parsing, and [htmx](https://htmx.org/) for partial-page swaps.
- **`css/styles.css`** â€“ single stylesheet; uses CSS custom properties (`--color-*`, `--spacing-*`, `--radius-*`).
- **`_site/`** â€“ generated output; do not edit files here directly.

### Key CSS Classes for Tags

| Class | Where used | Purpose |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| `.tag` | Tool detail pages | Prominent pill-style tag link |
| `.tags` | Tool detail pages | Container for `.tag` elements |
| `.card-tag` | Project cards | Small, subtle inline tag badge (non-interactive) |
| `.card-tags` | Project cards | Container for `.card-tag` elements |
| `.tag-cloud-item` | Index page tag cloud | Tag cloud link in the footer section |
| `.tag-group-title` | Category grid view | Section heading per tag group |
